questions.transcript:
Question: Where is the meeting being held?
A. With the marketing team.
B. At three o'clock.
C. In the main conference room.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks where the meeting is being held, so a place is needed. Choice C gives a place: the main conference room.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 회의가 어디에서 열리는지 묻고 있으므로 장소가 필요합니다. C는 장소인 main conference room을 제시합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Where 질문은 장소를 묻습니다. 시간이나 사람/팀을 말하는 응답은 자연스럽지 않습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The meeting is being held in the main conference room.
The meeting is in the main conference room.
The marketing team will attend the meeting.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: マーケティングチーム

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: The meeting is being held in the main conference room.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C です。質問は「Where is the meeting being held?」で、場所を求めています。正しい応答「In the main conference room.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Where is the meeting being held?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Have you finished the report yet?
A. Yes, I sent it this morning.
B. It's on the desk.
C. I work every day.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks whether the report has been finished. Choice A answers yes and explains that it was sent this morning.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 보고서를 끝냈는지 묻고 있습니다. A는 그렇다고 답하며 오늘 아침에 보냈다고 설명합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Have you finished...? 는 완료 여부를 묻는 질문입니다. Yes/No와 함께 완료된 행동을 설명하는 응답이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Have you finished the report yet?
The report was sent this morning.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Have you finished the report yet?

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A です。質問は「Have you finished the report yet?」で、Yes/No または間接的な確認を求めています。正しい応答「Yes, I sent it this morning.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Have you finished the report yet?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Could you take a message for me?
A. Sure, what should I write down?
B. The message was clear.
C. I sent an email yesterday.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question is a request to take a message. Choice A accepts the request and asks what should be written down.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 메시지를 받아 달라는 요청입니다. A는 요청을 수락하고 무엇을 적어야 하는지 묻습니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Could you...? 는 정중한 요청입니다. 요청을 받아들이거나 필요한 정보를 묻는 응답이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Could you take a message for me?

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Could you take a message for me?

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A です。質問は「Could you take a message for me?」で、依頼への応答を求めています。正しい応答「Sure, what should I write down?」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Could you take a message for me?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Why was the meeting canceled?
A. With our clients.
B. In the afternoon.
C. Because the manager was sick.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks why the meeting was canceled, so a reason is needed. Choice C gives the reason: the manager was sick.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 회의가 왜 취소되었는지 묻고 있으므로 이유가 필요합니다. C는 매니저가 아팠다는 이유를 제시합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Why 질문은 이유를 묻습니다. Because로 시작하는 응답은 이유를 제시할 때 자주 쓰입니다.
questions.explanationJa: 正解は C です。質問は「Why was the meeting canceled?」で、理由を求めています。正しい応答「Because the manager was sick.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Why was the meeting canceled?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Do you accept credit cards?
A. On the counter.
B. Yes, we do.
C. For two hours.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks whether credit cards are accepted. Choice B directly answers yes.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 신용카드를 받는지 묻고 있습니다. B는 그렇다고 직접 답합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Do you...? 로 시작하는 Yes/No 질문입니다. 장소나 시간 표현보다 yes/no 응답이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.meaningJa: クレジットカードを受け付ける

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The card reader is on the counter.
The store was closed for two hours.

vocabularyItem.meaningJa: クレジットカードを受け付ける

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「Do you accept credit cards?」で、Yes/No または間接的な確認を求めています。正しい応答「Yes, we do.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Do you accept credit cards?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Who is in charge of this project?
A. In the office.
B. Next week.
C. Ms. Williams is.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks who is responsible for the project. Choice C names the person in charge: Ms. Williams.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 이 프로젝트의 담당자가 누구인지 묻고 있습니다. C는 담당자인 Ms. Williams를 말합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Who 질문은 사람을 묻습니다. 장소나 시간 표현은 이 질문에 맞지 않습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Who is in charge of this project?
Ms. Williams is in charge of the project.

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Who is in charge of this project?

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C です。質問は「Who is in charge of this project?」で、人を求めています。正しい応答「Ms. Williams is.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Who is in charge of this project?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Should we postpone the presentation?
A. It was very interesting.
B. That might be a good idea.
C. On the second floor.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question suggests postponing the presentation. Choice B responds naturally by agreeing that it might be a good idea.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 발표를 연기할지 제안하고 있습니다. B는 좋은 생각일 수 있다고 자연스럽게 응답합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Should we...? 는 제안이나 의견 요청입니다. 동의하거나 반대하는 응답이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: That might be a good idea.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「Should we postpone the presentation?」で、提案への反応を求めています。正しい応答「That might be a good idea.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Should we postpone the presentation?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: What seems to be the problem with the computer?
A. It keeps shutting down.
B. On my desk.
C. Yesterday afternoon.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks what the computer problem is. Choice A describes the problem: it keeps shutting down.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 컴퓨터에 어떤 문제가 있는지 묻고 있습니다. A는 계속 꺼진다는 문제를 설명합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: What is the problem...? 은 문제의 내용을 묻습니다. 장소나 시간보다 증상 설명이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: What seems to be the problem with the computer?
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: コンピューターの問題

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: What seems to be the problem with the computer?
vocabularyItem.meaningJa: コンピューターの問題

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A です。質問は「What seems to be the problem with the computer?」で、質問の機能を求めています。正しい応答「It keeps shutting down.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「What seems to be the problem with the computer?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Where did you put my passport?
A. At the airport.
B. In your bag.
C. With my colleague.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks where the passport was put. Choice B gives a specific location: in your bag.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 여권을 어디에 두었는지 묻고 있습니다. B는 구체적인 위치인 가방 안을 말합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Where did you put...? 은 물건의 위치를 묻습니다. 가장 자연스러운 위치 표현을 고르세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Where did you put my passport?
I put it in your bag.
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: あなたのかばんの中に

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Where did you put my passport?

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「Where did you put my passport?」で、場所を求めています。正しい応答「In your bag.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Where did you put my passport?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Did you receive my email?
A. In the inbox.
B. It's very important.
C. Yes, I replied this morning.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks whether the email was received. Choice C answers yes and says the speaker replied this morning.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 이메일을 받았는지 묻고 있습니다. C는 그렇다고 답하며 오늘 아침에 답장했다고 말합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Did you...? 는 과거 행동 여부를 묻는 Yes/No 질문입니다. Yes/No와 이어지는 행동 설명을 들으세요.

vocabularyItems.meaningJa: 私のメールを受け取る

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The message is in the inbox.

vocabularyItem.meaningJa: 私のメールを受け取る

questions.explanationJa: 正解は C です。質問は「Did you receive my email?」で、Yes/No または間接的な確認を求めています。正しい応答「Yes, I replied this morning.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Did you receive my email?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Can I get the bill, please?
A. Of course, I'll bring it right away.
B. It's very delicious.
C. At the next table.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question is a polite request for the bill. Choice A accepts the request and says the bill will be brought right away.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 계산서를 달라는 정중한 요청입니다. A는 요청을 수락하고 바로 가져오겠다고 말합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: Can I get...? 은 식당이나 서비스 상황에서 자주 쓰는 요청 표현입니다. 요청에 응하는 응답이 자연스럽습니다.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Can I get the bill, please?

vocabularyItem.exampleSentence: Can I get the bill, please?

questions.explanationJa: 正解は A です。質問は「Can I get the bill, please?」で、質問の機能を求めています。正しい応答「Of course, I'll bring it right away.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Can I get the bill, please?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: When does the train arrive?
A. On platform five.
B. In about ten minutes.
C. With two bags.
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: The question asks when the train arrives. Choice B gives a time: in about ten minutes.
questions.explanationKo: 질문은 기차가 언제 도착하는지 묻고 있습니다. B는 약 10분 후라는 시간을 제시합니다.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: When 질문은 시간을 묻습니다. 플랫폼이나 동반 물건보다 시간 표현을 고르세요.

vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: The train arrives in ten minutes.
It arrives in about ten minutes.

questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「When does the train arrive?」で、時間を求めています。正しい応答「In about ten minutes.」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「When does the train arrive?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Who approved the budget proposal?
A. At the end of the hallway
B. The finance manager did
C. Nearly fifty dollars
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: Choice B is the best response in the audio.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 첫 단어를 특히 강하게 들어야 합니다. "Who approved the budget proposal?"에서는 질문이 무엇을 요구하는지 정한 뒤 응답을 고르면 비슷한 단어 함정에 덜 흔들립니다.

vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: The finance manager did
핵심 표현: Who approved the budget proposal
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: 誰が予算案を承認したか

vocabularyItem.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: The finance manager did

questions.explanationKo: 정답은 B입니다. 질문은 "Who approved the budget proposal?"이고, 사람을 요구합니다. 정답 응답 "The finance manager did"은 그 기능에 직접 답합니다. Part 2에서는 비슷한 단어가 들리는지보다 질문이 장소, 시간, 요청, 제안, 이유 중 무엇을 묻는지 먼저 판단하는 것이 중요합니다.
questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「Who approved the budget proposal?」で、人を求めています。正しい応答「The finance manager did」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Who approved the budget proposal?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Should we order more office supplies?
A. I prefer blue pens
B. On the wooden table
C. Yes, we're running low
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: Choice C is the best response in the audio.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 첫 단어를 특히 강하게 들어야 합니다. "Should we order more office supplies?"에서는 질문이 무엇을 요구하는지 정한 뒤 응답을 고르면 비슷한 단어 함정에 덜 흔들립니다.

vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: Yes, we're running low
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: はい、残りが少なくなっています

vocabularyItems.word: Should we order more office supplies
vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: Should we order more office supplies
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: もっと事務用品を注文すべきですか
vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Should we order more office supplies

vocabularyItem.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: Yes, we're running low
vocabularyItem.meaningJa: はい、残りが少なくなっています

questions.explanationKo: 정답은 C입니다. 질문은 "Should we order more office supplies?"이고, 제안에 대한 반응을 요구합니다. 정답 응답 "Yes, we're running low"은 그 기능에 직접 답합니다. Part 2에서는 비슷한 단어가 들리는지보다 질문이 장소, 시간, 요청, 제안, 이유 중 무엇을 묻는지 먼저 판단하는 것이 중요합니다.
questions.explanationJa: 正解は C です。質問は「Should we order more office supplies?」で、提案への反応を求めています。正しい応答「Yes, we're running low」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Should we order more office supplies?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Where should I park my car?
A. I usually drive to work
B. In the underground garage
C. It's a very small vehicle
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: Choice B is the best response in the audio.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 첫 단어를 특히 강하게 들어야 합니다. "Where should I park my car?"에서는 질문이 무엇을 요구하는지 정한 뒤 응답을 고르면 비슷한 단어 함정에 덜 흔들립니다.

vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: In the underground garage

vocabularyItems.word: Where should I park my car
vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: Where should I park my car
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: どこに車を駐車すればよいですか
vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: Where should I park my car

vocabularyItem.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: In the underground garage

questions.explanationKo: 정답은 B입니다. 질문은 "Where should I park my car?"이고, 장소을 요구합니다. 정답 응답 "In the underground garage"은 그 기능에 직접 답합니다. Part 2에서는 비슷한 단어가 들리는지보다 질문이 장소, 시간, 요청, 제안, 이유 중 무엇을 묻는지 먼저 판단하는 것이 중요합니다.
questions.explanationJa: 正解は B です。質問は「Where should I park my car?」で、場所を求めています。正しい応答「In the underground garage」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。

questions.transcript:

questions.transcript:

questions.transcript:
Question: How often do you update the website?
A. Nearly every week
B. With the software engineer
C. At the front desk
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: Choice A is the best response in the audio.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 첫 단어를 특히 강하게 들어야 합니다. "How often do you update the website?"에서는 질문이 무엇을 요구하는지 정한 뒤 응답을 고르면 비슷한 단어 함정에 덜 흔들립니다.

vocabularyItems.word: How often do you update the website
vocabularyItems.meaningKo: 핵심 표현: How often do you update the website
vocabularyItems.meaningJa: どのくらいの頻度でウェブサイトを更新しますか
vocabularyItems.exampleSentence: How often do you update the website

questions.explanationKo: 정답은 A입니다. 질문은 "How often do you update the website?"이고, 방법이나 상태을 요구합니다. 정답 응답 "Nearly every week"은 그 기능에 직접 답합니다. Part 2에서는 비슷한 단어가 들리는지보다 질문이 장소, 시간, 요청, 제안, 이유 중 무엇을 묻는지 먼저 판단하는 것이 중요합니다.
questions.explanationJa: 正解は A です。質問は「How often do you update the website?」で、方法や状態を求めています。正しい応答「Nearly every week」はその機能に直接答えています。Part 2では単語の一致より、質問が場所・時間・依頼・提案・理由のどれを求めているかを先に判断します。
questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「How often do you update the website?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。

questions.transcript:
Question: Can you send me the updated schedule?

C. With the accounting team
questions.prompt: Select the best response to the question.
questions.explanationEn: Choice A is the best response in the audio.
questions.koreanLearnerHint: 첫 단어를 특히 강하게 들어야 합니다. "Can you send me the updated schedule?"에서는 질문이 무엇을 요구하는지 정한 뒤 응답을 고르면 비슷한 단어 함정에 덜 흔들립니다.

questions.japaneseLearnerHint: 最初の語句を特に強く聞きます。「Can you send me the updated schedule?」では、質問が何を求めているかを決めてから応答を選ぶと、似た単語のひっかけに流されにくくなります。
